label: hate
rationale: 'LUOs are very cheap' attributes collective mercenary character to the entire Luo community as a group - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Condemns a specific mob-justice killing and criticises belief in witchcraft; no attack on an ethnic group as a group, though 'some Africans are savages' is sweeping.

label: hate
rationale: 'Entitled kikuyus' twice attributed collective guilt for 2007 PEV, declaring Kalenjins do not need Kikuyus and their 'deception' - transfers individual political acts onto the whole community.

label: neither
rationale: Criticises Luo community for alleged tribalism but frames it as a counter-accusation within inter-ethnic political debate; more rhetorical point-scoring than collective contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'garbage', 'totally useless', 'ujinga' aimed at named individual Bishop Wanjiru and her son - personal abuse with no ethnic targeting.

label: neither
rationale: Retweet of a radio caller's account of land grabbing and internal Kalenjin political criticism; reporting of events and intra-community critique, no collective contempt directed at a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Luos are cheap they will be bought at bei ya jioni' - collective attribution of political venality to the entire Luo community.

label: neither
rationale: Confusing mix of accusations about goons and witchcraft directed at individuals or vague referents; no clear collective ethnic attack, though 'witch people' and the Kisii reference are borderline.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wamunyoro is primitive' - collective derogatory characterisation of a community as primitive.

label: neither
rationale: Ethnic-bloc electoral analysis: 'MERUS WILL NOT FOLLOW KIKUYUS BLINDLY' and Mt Kenya bloc fragmentation - voting arithmetic with no contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Luhya voters distancing themselves from Luo Nyanza coalition - inter-community political realignment talk, no collective contempt or dehumanisation.

label: hate
rationale: 'evil Kikuyu axis of power' transfers Martha Karua's alleged role in 2007 PEV onto the Kikuyu community as a collective, attributing evil to the group.

label: hate
rationale: 'deport every last muslim' and 'Send them back to Shitganistan' - explicit expulsion call against Muslims as a group with derogatory slur.

label: hate
rationale: 'Tutam luo brokers' collectively attributes brokerage/exploitation with zero development to Luo politicians, sliding toward group-level contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'bukusus from chwele... total clowns, they always do whatever wetangula asks' - collective characterisation of Bukusu people as clowns and blindly obedient.

label: neither
rationale: General lament that Kenyan politics is dominated by tribalism across all parties; observation of ethnic patronage without attributing contemptible characteristics to any specific group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Entitled kikuyus fraudsters' collectively attributes fraud and deception to the Kikuyu community, holding the whole group responsible for elite political acts.

label: neither
rationale: Criticism of named Luo political leaders for sending goons to surround a church - targets leaders' conduct, not the Luo community as a group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'wakabila wakubwa', 'hawafai kuongoza' directed at three named politicians individually - harsh political criticism of individuals, no collective ethnic contempt transferred to their communities.

label: hate
rationale: 'Watu ka hawa wapigwe viboko' calls for physical violence against Luhya people as a group for their political choices.

label: neither
rationale: Blames a named individual 'Kasongo' for inciting division and references Kofi Annan violence statistics - targets one person's conduct, no group-level contempt.

label: neither
rationale: 'Adui wa Wakenya ni wawili, shetani na Ruto' - hyperbolic political criticism of Ruto as an individual, no ethnic group targeted.

label: hate
rationale: 'kick these Somalians out of kenya' is an explicit expulsion call against Somalis as an ethnic/national-origin group.

label: neither
rationale: Multi-community electoral coalition arithmetic ('we are not supporting another luo', 'Wakamba', 'Kikuyus ni unpredictable') - voting-bloc analysis with no collective contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'ur stupid shia bastards' attributes collective stupidity and manipulation to Shia Muslims as a religious group - ethnic/religious contempt with dehumanising insult.
